titlestmt.titleproper: Cornell University banners, clothing, and other textiles47-7-3439
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-10 23:59:09 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University banners, clothing, and other textiles
did.physdesc: Memorabilia.
did.abstract: Banners, pennants, pillows, runners, and other cloth items.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: See Stephen J. Roberts Papers, #24-2-2903, for an additional banner.
See also Cigarette silk collection, #6612, for additional Cornell University related textiles.
See Ruth Lorena Gates Fisher papers, #37-5-4496, for a small felt pennant (MU-4024).
See Henry P. de Forest papers, #3214, for an orange Cornell pennant (MU-1176).

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Banners, Clothing, and Other Textiles, #47-7-3439. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Banners, pennants, pillows, runners, jackets, blazers, reunion items, graduation robes and regalia, stuffed animal mascots, and other cloth items. Examples include a Cornell University doctoral hood, originally owned by Dr. William J. Edens, Ph.D. 1937 and Class of 1941 blazer owned by Arthur Townsend Klinger.

did.unittitle: Series I: Banners, Pennants, and Flags

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American Knitwear, brand name used by pennant manufacturer Keezer

did.unittitle: Large Cornell flag, probably owned by John Sherwood Hoffmire, Class of 1916

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chicago Pennant Company.

did.unittitle: Cornell banner, red with white lettering
Cornell banner with "Cornell" written on each end and raised emblem in center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shown as part of the Sesquicentennial Exhibition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advertised in the Alumni News as avilable at Rothschild Brothers.

did.unittitle: Cornell red felt banner with leather emblem in center
Cornell pennant with printed emblem and letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This football player illustration was used for pennants of other schools as well

did.unittitle: Cornell pennant with emblem and red lettering on white
Red felt banner with "Cornell" in white

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collegiate Manufacturing Company

did.unittitle: Cornell pennant belonging to Dr. Alexander Powell Hyde, Class of 1945

did.physdesc: Rectangular red felt banner with yellow and gray logo.

did.unittitle: Large felt banner made by Rothschild Bros, Ithaca
did.physdesc: 68 inches long by 33 inches wide.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collegiate Manufacturing Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red felt banner with white felt letters. Approximately 68 x 34 inches.

did.unittitle: Red felt pennant with "Cornell" and bear leaning against a "C"
Cornell banner with diagonal felt "Cornell", emblem, and felt "CU"
Subseries Ib. Cornell Class Banners and Pennants
Red banner with white "'84", class of 1884
White canvas banner with "1893" in red
Gold banner with "Cornell '95" in blue letters and vice versa
White canvas banner with "1896" in red
White canvas banner with "1897" in red
White canvas banner with "1901" in red
White canvas banner with "1902" in red
White canvas banner with "1903" in red
White canvas banner with "1904" in red
White canvas banner with "1905" in red
White canvas banner with "1906" in red
White canvas banner with "1907" in red
White canvas banner with "1908" in red
White canvas banner with "1909" in red
White canvas banner with "1910" in red
White canvas banner with "1911" in red
White canvas banner with "1912" in red
Green banner with "1920s here" in white
White canvas banner with "1920" in red
White canvas banner with "1923" in red
White canvas banner with "1924" in red
White canvas banner with "1925" in red
White canvas banner with "1926" in red
White canvas banner with "1928" in red
Red felt 1935 banner with leather emblem
White canvas banner with "1937" in red
White canvas banner with "1938" in red
White canvas banner with "1939" in red
Brown felt banner with "1939" in yellow
White canvas banner with "1941" in red
Red canvas banner with "1941" in white
Red felt "1941" banner with leather emblem
did.physdesc: MU-4132 has moth damage. MU-4133 has heavy wear on the leather emblem.

did.unittitle: White canvas banner with "1942" in red
Red felt Class of 1942 banner with leather emblem
Black felt banner with "1942" in plaid

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two identical banners, but MU-2439 has a CU button and football pin in the center

did.physdesc: Red felt pennant with white felt letters, made by Chicago Pennant Co. 44.5 inches long by 16.5 inches wide.

did.unittitle: Class of 1954 silk scarf with image of the Clock Tower
Felt pennants on sticks for various classes
Cornell 1942 class banner with leather emblem
Cornell class of 2011 red and white felt banner
Subseries Ic. Cornell Clubs, Associations, and Departments
Banner – "When Better Women are Made Cornell Men Will Make Them"
Track and Field silk banner with metal tassles
Small banner - "Baker Court, Souvenir of Ithaca"
Cayuga Club Banner, a club that later became a chapter of the Sigma Phi Sigma Fraternity

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dark red felt banner with white felt letters. Approximately 58 x 36 inches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Possibly associated with banner MU-4747

did.unittitle: Series II: Pillows and Decorative Items
Laundry bag given to freshman in 1952
Patchwork quilt by Dilbert H. Decker, class of 1884
Red Wool Felt Pillow with "Cornell" in white
Felt Cornell pillow cover owned by Guy Gundaker
2 pillowcases with red satin trim, embroidered wheel and signatures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: MU-533: Signature of women
MU-534: Signatures of men with date of graduation

did.unittitle: Red and white felt pillow cover
Red cloth napkins with Cornell University logo printed on them
Red cloth table runner with Cornell
Hand-embroidered pillow case with the Cornell insignia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letter "G" is embroidered on verso.

did.physdesc: Beige pillow with Cornell embroidered in red. Red rope lines the edges with red tassels in upper left corner.

did.unittitle: Pillow, "It's hard to be humble when you're from Cornell"
Red felt Cornell pillow with leather emblem
Subseries IIIa. Blazers, Jackets, and sweaters
Red and white striped seersucker blazer
White jacket with red stripes down the zipper
White knit athletic sweater for tennis
Red cotton blazer with white trim
Kenneth C. Newman, Class of 1910, reunion jacket
Kenneth C. Newman, Class of 1910, reunion hat
Class of 1915 white sweater with red felt numbers
Class of 1916 purple blazer with 1916 button

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Eggplant purple blazer with white trim and a 1916 reunion button.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red blazer with white "18" on pocket.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red reunion jacket with embroidered "19" on breast pocket.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red blazer with white circle on back, embroidered with red "21"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: White cotton button-down jacket with painted red "'21" on back and breast pocket.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: White cotton pullover jacket with salmon-colored accents and white print "'21" on pocket.

did.physdesc: White blazer with red "25" stitched on breast pocket.
Red, white, and blue striped jacket with red "25" on pocket.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: White, red, and blue checkered blazer with embroidered crest on pocket.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: White, red, orange, and yellow striped blazer with red felt "29" on pocket.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: White jacket with two embroidered billiards balls on the back, one with a "2" and one with a "9"

did.physdesc: White blazer with red trim.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cream blazer with red trim.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red blazer with white felt "38" on pocket. Likely a reunion jacket.

did.unittitle: Class of 1941 blazer owned by Arthur Townsend Klinger

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cream blazer with dark red trim and emblem on pocket.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cream blazer with dark red trim and emblem on pocket.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red jacket with white "42" painted on the back.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pale blue blazer with white trim and white felt "43" on pocket.

custodhist.p: Formerly owned by Richard R. Nickerson.

did.physdesc: Red blazer with cream "46" on pocket.

custodhist.p: Owned by John W. Drew.

did.physdesc: Red jacket with red "'47" on back.

custodhist.p: Previously owned by Frank Rhodes.

scopecontent.p: Red parka, likely form the 50th class reunion.

custodhist.p: Formerly owned by Douglas Anderson, class of 1950.

did.physdesc: Cream jacket with red trim and embroidered seal on pocket. Made by Robert Rollins Blazers.
Charcoal gray jacket with gold trim and embroidered seal on pocket. Made by Robert Rollins Blazers.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Previously owned by Patricia Marlowe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: White jacket with red trim and a printed red "C" with Cornell emblem on right breast.

did.unittitle: Delta Chi, Roman Holiday '55 BC jacket

did.physdesc: White t-shirt with a red bear drinking beer captioned "hey Harvard."

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Formerly owned by Frank T. Rhodes.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Previously owned by Frank T. Rhodes.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Previously owned by Frank T. Rhodes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two robes one with Cornell seal embroidered on a field of blue, hoods, and mortar boards. One cap has initials BLR inside. Some of these items were worn while Rideout led President Frank Rhodes' 1977 inauguration procession.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Previously owned by Frank T. Rhodes.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Previously owned by Frank T. Rhodes.

did.physdesc: Red button-down shirt with white "25" painted on breast pocket.

scopecontent.p: Red vest with a bear and "C 51" on the back.

PhD hood with blue velvet lining
Armband with "C '04" button, belonging to Morris Buck
Armband, "04", white felt with red numbers

scopecontent.p: Red silk scarf with "C27" printed in corner.

did.unittitle: White scarf with red Cornell insignia

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Owned by Marjorie Cornell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 52" by 36" piece of white-dotted indigo percale, with label depicting Cornell Crew member standing on dock. Manufactured by W.P. Co.

did.unittitle: Red sesquicentennial scarf created by the Johnson School
Ivy League Cornell Champions red bandana
Checkered white and green silk tie with matching "04" button

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: White belt with red "Cornell '54"

did.unittitle: Series IV: Patches, Badges, and Tags
Kenneth C. Newman, Class of 1910, white leather square with straps
Series V. Mascots and stuffed animals

did.physdesc: Tan bear wearing red arm band with a white "C" on it.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Former owner, Frank Putnam Deane.

did.physdesc: Red felt dog with white jacket and red "C"
White fleece elephant with red '26 pennant. Both ears are detached.
Small fleece elephant. One ear is detached.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Likely a Class of 1926 mascot.

did.physdesc: Red and white felt elephant with "Cornell" written on both ears and '26 embroidered on its right side.
Stuffed white dog with painted red fur and "XXX" barrel around neck.
Red bean-bag bear, with Cornell printed on chest and tongue sticking out.
Red and white felt turtle with bonnet.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Former owner Eleanor Jean Kahles Newton, Class of 1949.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Yellow and blue stuffed goat with small ribbon around its neck.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manufactured in Syracuse, New York. Printed Cornell insignia on both ears.

did.physdesc: Red "C" on ears, missing tail.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Former owner: John Brennan, class of 1952

did.unittitle: Sigma Phi Epsilon red dog mascot

did.unittitle: Class of 1947 blue drawstring bag with yellow "'47"
Cornell Association of Class Officers (CACO) 100 years of Class Leadership tote bag
